articles.title: She’s running Boston to honor her mother’s fight against cancer
articles.description:
"This race is more than 26.2 miles — it is a tribute to my mom’s courage and a thank-you to the team that helped save her life"

The post She’s running Boston to honor her mother’s fight against cancer appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Allyson Bogdanski runs the Boston Marathon to honor her mother’s cancer battle.
The race symbolizes gratitude for the medical team at Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.
Bogdanski aims to inspire others facing similar challenges in Boston.

articles.title: GoFundMe for Boston officer charged in fatal shooting soars past $400K
articles.description:
The state’s police oversight board suspended Officer Nicholas O'Malley last week after he was charged in the March 11 shooting death of Stephenson King.

The post GoFundMe for Boston officer charged in fatal shooting soars past $400K appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Officer Nicholas O'Malley was suspended after being charged in Stephenson King's shooting.
GoFundMe for O'Malley has raised over $400,000 since the incident.
The case raises concerns about police accountability in Boston.

articles.title: AI attack ads are hitting Massachusetts political campaigns
articles.description:
Lawmakers are considering new legislation to regulate the use of AI in political advertising. Have you spotted them?

The post AI attack ads are hitting Massachusetts political campaigns appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Lawmakers in Massachusetts are considering legislation to regulate AI in political ads.
AI-generated attack ads are increasingly appearing in local campaigns ahead of the 2026 elections.
Proposed regulations aim to protect voters from misleading information in political advertising.
